section.heading: Internships of United States nationals at international organizations

content.p: The Secretary is authorized to bolster efforts to increase the number of United States citizens representative of the American people occupying positions in the United Nations system, agencies, and commissions, and in other international organizations, including by awarding grants to educational institutions and students.

subsection.heading: Report
subsection.chapeau: Not later than 90 days after December 22, 2023, the Secretary of State shall submit a report to the appropriate congressional committees that identifies—

paragraph.content: the number of United States citizens who are involved in internship programs at international organizations;
the distribution of the individuals described in paragraph (1) among various international organizations; and
grants, programs, and other activities that are being utilized to recruit and fund United States citizens to participate in internship programs at international organizations.

subsection.heading: Eligibility
subsection.chapeau: An individual referred to in subsection (a) is an individual who—

paragraph.chapeau: is enrolled at or received their degree within two years from—

subparagraph.content: an institution of higher education; or
an institution of higher education based outside the United States, as determined by the Secretary; and

paragraph.content: is a citizen of the United States.

content.p: There is authorized to be appropriated $1,500,000 for the Department for fiscal year 2024 to carry out the grant program authorized under subsection (a).

note.heading: Definitions
note.p: For definitions of “Secretary”, “appropriate congressional committees”, and “Department” as used in this section, see section 6002 of Pub. L. 118–31, set out as a note under section 2651 of this title.
